sections.markdown: # Intro to Grail and DQL - Part 2

# Exercise BONUS:  Advanced Exercises (Optional)

This is an OPTIONAL set of BONUS exercises. 

Istio Logs processing

## Please duplicate the Notebook before editing it. If you need instructions, check out the Lab Guide. Thanks!


The following environemnt use the OpenTelemetry Demo with Istio . Meaning that each OpenTelemetry Service has a Sidecar Proxy producing Access Log for each:
* incoming 
* and outgoing request

From the Istio Logs lots of powerful information could be extracted to troubleshoot networking issues.

The proxy used by Istio is Envoy. Envoy is producing Accesslogs exported with OpenTelmeetry log format.

## Envoy Access Log Format
To be able to parse the access logs stored in Grail , let's have a look at the Envoy Access log format: 
https://istio.io/latest/docs/tasks/observability/logs/access-log/#default-access-log-format
```
[%START_TIME%] \"%REQ(:METHOD)% %REQ(X-ENVOY-ORIGINAL-PATH?:PATH)% %PROTOCOL%\" %RESPONSE_CODE% %RESPONSE_FLAGS% %RESPONSE_CODE_DETAILS% %CONNECTION_TERMINATION_DETAILS%
\"%UPSTREAM_TRANSPORT_FAILURE_REASON%\" %BYTES_RECEIVED% %BYTES_SENT% %DURATION% %RESP(X-ENVOY-UPSTREAM-SERVICE-TIME)% \"%REQ(X-FORWARDED-FOR)%\" \"%REQ(USER-AGENT)%\" \"%REQ(X-REQUEST-ID)%\"
\"%REQ(:AUTHORITY)%\" \"%UPSTREAM_HOST%\" %UPSTREAM_CLUSTER_RAW% %UPSTREAM_LOCAL_ADDRESS% %DOWNSTREAM_LOCAL_ADDRESS% %DOWNSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS% %REQUESTED_SERVER_NAME% %ROUTE_NAME%\n
```

##### Exercise B.1

Let's start by filtering our logs to visualize this format.

Create a DQL Query filtering :
* `log.filepath` is null ( because the logs is sent using the opentelemetry protocol) 
* `service.version` ( part of the opentelemtry semantic convention

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter isNull(log.file.path)
| filter isNull(service.version )

sections.markdown:
##### Exercise B.2

From this result we can see that the `content` field stores the accesslog of envoy.
Let's parse it to get all the details from envoy following the envoy access log format: 
```
[%START_TIME%] \"%REQ(:METHOD)% %REQ(X-ENVOY-ORIGINAL-PATH?:PATH)% %PROTOCOL%\" %RESPONSE_CODE% %RESPONSE_FLAGS% %RESPONSE_CODE_DETAILS% %CONNECTION_TERMINATION_DETAILS%
\"%UPSTREAM_TRANSPORT_FAILURE_REASON%\" %BYTES_RECEIVED% %BYTES_SENT% %DURATION% %RESP(X-ENVOY-UPSTREAM-SERVICE-TIME)% \"%REQ(X-FORWARDED-FOR)%\" \"%REQ(USER-AGENT)%\" \"%REQ(X-REQUEST-ID)%\"
\"%REQ(:AUTHORITY)%\" \"%UPSTREAM_HOST%\" %UPSTREAM_CLUSTER_RAW% %UPSTREAM_LOCAL_ADDRESS% %DOWNSTREAM_LOCAL_ADDRESS% %DOWNSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS% %REQUESTED_SERVER_NAME% %ROUTE_NAME%\n
```

Note to make the parsing easier you can Parse the content with 2 parsing instruction.
```
| parse content , "LD:test SPACE SQS:downstream LD:rest_of_content"
| parse rest_of_content, "..."
```

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter isNull(log.file.path)
| filter isNull(service.version )
| parse content ,"LD:intro SPACE DQS:downstream SPACE+ INT:istio.responsecode SPACE LD:istion.response_flag SPACE LD:istion.response_reason SPACE  LD:istio.response_condition SPACE DQS SPACE INT:istio.bytereceived SPACE INT:istio.bytesent SPACE INT:istio.duration SPACE (INT:istio.upstreamServertime | LD:istio.upstreamservertime) SPACE DQS:istio.forwardedfor SPACE DQS:istio.useragent SPACE DQS:istio.requestid SPACE DQS:istio.authority SPACE DQS:istio.upstreamhost SPACE LD:istio.upstream_cluster SPACE LD:istio.upstream_local SPACE LD:istio.downstream_local SPACE LD:istio.downstream_remote SPACE LD:istio.rest "
| parse downstream,"LD:istio.method SPACE LD:istio.uri SPACE LD:istio.httpversion"
| parse istio.upstream_cluster, "LD:istio.type '|' LD:port '||' "

sections.markdown:
##### Exercise B.3

From the field extacted we can see that we have URLS.
Lots of URLs are related to the same service but they only differ through the id used in the url.

let's create a istio.simplified_url field that will have a simplified rule , where all the  id would be replaced by `{id}`
for example : `/api/products/1YMWWN1N4O` will become : `/api/products/{id}`

Note you will ned to use `if()`, `matchesPattern()` and `replacePattern`

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter isNull(log.file.path)
| filter isNull(service.version )
| parse content ,"LD:intro SPACE DQS:downstream SPACE+ INT:istio.responsecode SPACE LD:istion.response_flag SPACE LD:istion.response_reason SPACE  LD:istio.response_condition SPACE DQS SPACE INT:istio.bytereceived SPACE INT:istio.bytesent SPACE INT:istio.duration SPACE (INT:istio.upstreamServertime | LD:istio.upstreamservertime) SPACE DQS:istio.forwardedfor SPACE DQS:istio.useragent SPACE DQS:istio.requestid SPACE DQS:istio.authority SPACE DQS:istio.upstreamhost SPACE LD:istio.upstream_cluster SPACE LD:istio.upstream_local SPACE LD:istio.downstream_local SPACE LD:istio.downstream_remote SPACE LD:istio.rest "
| parse downstream,"LD:istio.method SPACE LD:istio.uri SPACE LD:istio.httpversion"
| parse istio.upstream_cluster, "LD:istio.type '|' LD:port '||' "
| fieldsRemove downstream
| parse istio.uri , "[^\\?]+:url"
| fieldsAdd istio.simplified_url= if(matchesPattern(url,"LD '.' [a-z]{2,3}"),"{staticontent}", else:replacePattern(url,"[A-Z0-9]{4,}","{id}" ))
| fields istio.authority, istio.bytereceived, istio.bytesent , istio.downstream_local , istio.downstream_remote , istio.duration, istio.forwardedfor , istio.httpversion , istio.method , istio.requestid , istio.response_condition, istio.responsecode, istio.simplified_url, istio.type, istio.upstreamServertime, istio.uri , istio.useragent, istion.response_flag, istion.response_reason , timestamp, dt.system.bucket , dt.system.sampling_ratio, dt.entity.cloud_application , dt.entity.cloud_application_instance, dt.entity.cloud_application_namespace, dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster , dt.kubernetes.cluster.id , dt.kubernetes.workload.name , dt.pipeline.source , k8s.cluster.name, k8s.deployment.name , k8s.namespace.name, k8s.node.name, k8s.pod.name, source, span_id, trace_id, dt.source_entity, dt.entity.service

sections.markdown:
##### Exercise B.4

Istio stores a `response flag` holding details about networking issues that could happen in our environemt.

the response flag is equal to "-" if everything is fine.

Let's filter our previous query on the traffic having issues.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter isNull(log.file.path)
| filter isNull(service.version )
| parse content ,"LD:intro SPACE DQS:downstream SPACE+ INT:istio.responsecode SPACE LD:istion.response_flag SPACE LD:istion.response_reason SPACE  LD:istio.response_condition SPACE DQS SPACE INT:istio.bytereceived SPACE INT:istio.bytesent SPACE INT:istio.duration SPACE (INT:istio.upstreamServertime | LD:istio.upstreamservertime) SPACE DQS:istio.forwardedfor SPACE DQS:istio.useragent SPACE DQS:istio.requestid SPACE DQS:istio.authority SPACE DQS:istio.upstreamhost SPACE LD:istio.upstream_cluster SPACE LD:istio.upstream_local SPACE LD:istio.downstream_local SPACE LD:istio.downstream_remote SPACE LD:istio.rest "
| parse downstream,"LD:istio.method SPACE LD:istio.uri SPACE LD:istio.httpversion"
| parse istio.upstream_cluster, "LD:istio.type '|' LD:port '||' "
| fieldsRemove downstream
| parse istio.uri , "[^\\?]+:url"
| fieldsAdd istio.simplified_url= if(matchesPattern(url,"LD '.' [a-z]{2,3}"),"{staticontent}", else:replacePattern(url,"[A-Z0-9]{4,}","{id}" ))
| fields istio.authority, istio.bytereceived, istio.bytesent , istio.downstream_local , istio.downstream_remote , istio.duration, istio.forwardedfor , istio.httpversion , istio.method , istio.requestid , istio.response_condition, istio.responsecode, istio.simplified_url, istio.type, istio.upstreamServertime, istio.uri , istio.useragent, istion.response_flag, istion.response_reason , timestamp, dt.system.bucket , dt.system.sampling_ratio, dt.entity.cloud_application , dt.entity.cloud_application_instance, dt.entity.cloud_application_namespace, dt.entity.kubernetes_cluster , dt.kubernetes.cluster.id , dt.kubernetes.workload.name , dt.pipeline.source , k8s.cluster.name, k8s.deployment.name , k8s.namespace.name, k8s.node.name, k8s.pod.name, source, span_id, trace_id, dt.source_entity, dt.entity.service
| filter istion.response_flag != "-"

sections.markdown:
Here are the explanation of each code returned in the response flag:



## HTTP and TCP

| Long name | Short name  | Description |
| -------- | ------- | -------- |
| NoHealthyUpstream | UH | No healthy upstream hosts in upstream cluster in addition to 503 response code.|
| UpstreamConnectionFailure |  UF |  Upstream connection failure in addition to 503 response code.| 
| UpstreamOverflow| UO |  Upstream overflow (circuit breaking) in addition to 503 response code.| 
| NoRouteFound |  NR |  No route configured for a given request in addition to 404 response code or no matching filter chain for a downstream connection.| 
| UpstreamRetryLimitExceeded | URX | The request was rejected because the upstream retry limit (HTTP) or maximum connect attempts (TCP) was reached.|
| NoClusterFound | NC | Upstream cluster not found.|
| DurationTimeout | DT | When a request or connection exceeded max_connection_duration or max_downstream_connection_duration.|

## HTTP only

| Long name | Short name  | Description |
| -------- | ------- | -------- |
| DownstreamConnectionTermination | DC | Downstream connection termination.|
| FailedLocalHealthCheck | LH | Local service failed health check request in addition to 503 response code.|
| UpstreamRequestTimeout | UT | Upstream request timeout in addition to 504 response code.|
| LocalReset | LR | Connection local reset in addition to 503 response code.|
| UpstreamRemoteReset | UR | Upstream remote reset in addition to 503 response code.| 
| UpstreamConnectionTermination | UC | Upstream connection termination in addition to 503 response code.|
| DelayInjected | DI | The request processing was delayed for a period specified via fault injection.|
| FaultInjected | FI | The request was aborted with a response code specified via fault injection. |
| RateLimited | RL  | The request was rate-limited locally by the HTTP rate limit filter in addition to 429 response code.|
| UnauthorizedExternalService | UAEX | The request was denied by the external authorization service.|
| RateLimitServiceError | RLSE | The request was rejected because there was an error in rate limit service.|
| InvalidEnvoyRequestHeaders | IH | The request was rejected because it set an invalid value for a strictly-checked header in addition to 400 response code.|
| StreamIdleTimeout | SI | Stream idle timeout in addition to 408 or 504 response code.|
| DownstreamProtocolError | DPE | The downstream request had an HTTP protocol error.
| UpstreamProtocolError | UPE | The upstream response had an HTTP protocol error. |
| UpstreamMaxStreamDurationReached | UMSDR | The upstream request reached max stream duration.|
| ResponseFromCacheFilter | RFCF | The response was served from an Envoy cache filter.|
| NoFilterConfigFound | NFCF | The request is terminated because filter configuration was not received within the permitted warming deadline.| 
| OverloadManagerTerminated | OM | Overload Manager terminated the request.| 
| DnsResolutionFailed | DF | The request was terminated due to DNS resolution failure. | 
| DropOverload | DO | The request was terminated in addition to 503 response code due to drop_overloads.| 
| DownstreamRemoteReset | DR | The response details are http2.remote_reset or http2.remote_refuse.| 
| UnconditionalDropOverload | UDO |The request was terminated in addition to 503 response code due to drop_overloads is set to 100%.|
